---
title: "Guilford Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Introduction to Point Set Topology via Linearly Ordered Spaces", "Boyd, J. R.; Gordh Jr., G. Rudy", "topology", "63", "Guilford College", "Mathematically speaking, the primary purpose of this guide is to outline a topological characterization of the real number line among all linearly ordered spaces. Pedagogically speaking, its primary purpose is to actively involve the student in doing, rather than watching, mathematics. This guide is also intended (1) to introduce the student to the axiomatic and deductive character of mathematics, (2) to help the student develop his/her critical reasoning ability, (3) to provide the student with an understanding of the real number line, and (4) to introduce the student to important concepts of topology and analysis.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./point-set-topology_boyd_gordh.pdf)

This course guide was originally published by the Guilford College Journal of Undergraduate Mathematics as a volume in the Monographs on Undergraduate Mathematics series.

This course guide may be reproduced and distributed if the following conditions are met:

- State that it is the property of the Math Department of Guilford College, and is protected under copyright by Guilford.
- Take no profit from its distribution. (You may recover copying costs if you are using these for a class.)
